user_outcome: Planner and MCP adapters apply ratified deltas and pristine migrations through one engine authority that seeds the exact owner track, preserves lifecycle authority, and commits, propagates, and activates only the authorized edge atomically.
rationale: Requirements meaning remains a human Planner decision. C-12 makes only the mechanical application authoritative and restart-safe, and proves the first eligible seven-slice migration exactly without fabricating v0.15 execution evidence. The live downstream records are migrated only after this conformance release is integrated and its engine is current authority.
in_scope: Embed byte-identical production copies of the referenced strict plan-delta-v1 and protocol-migration-receipt-v1 planning schemas. A human-ratified delta contains every exact source, owner seed, byte mutation, rollback link, target local/remote compare-and-swap head pair, receipt path, and ratifier identity with a preserved numeric RFC3339 offset; mutation paths cannot enter plan-deltas, original and rollback slice IDs are each unique across rollback links, and receipt_path must differ from every mutation and canonical delta path. Target local/remote heads must match except for the exact validated C-08/S15 one-parent local-only invalidation topology. Transaction state is derived from Git topology and never caller-asserted.

Expose current-authority sworn replan apply --delta <canonical-json> for operation replan, plus activation-policy sworn replan migrate --delta <canonical-json> and sworn replan activate --delta <canonical-json> for the two protocol_migration edges; replace direct MCP planning-record writes with apply_plan_delta, migrate_plan_delta, and activate_migrated_plan tools that call the same corresponding engine operations.

Seed any started-slice status mutation from the exact oracle-resolved owner-track commit, validate full C-05/C-06 history, preserve maintainability byte-for-byte except adding an explicitly ratified rollback_slice_id, commit release records atomically to release-wt, and propagate canonical two-parent target commits derived from that release-wt transaction to affected track refs under Baton's conflict rules. For an exact S15 local-only invalidation, preserve that commit as target parent 1 and compare-and-swap the unchanged remote from its separately declared parent; every other target requires identical local and remote expected heads.

Commit one immutable receipt with source, target, and before/after blob identities; construct every unsigned Git object from the exact tree, ordered parents, fixed author/committer, Unix-seconds-plus-preserved-offset date bytes, and subject-only LF-terminated message in section 11; then derive not_started, locally_committed, fully_propagated, or divergent exclusively from those reconstructable objects so restart creates or pushes only missing canonical commits without replaying Planner judgment.

Support in-place migration only when every selected slice is planned with start_commit null, empty actual_files and maintainability.reports, null adjudication and rollback, and pending verification; apply the complete referenced 17-mutation local-first delta—including the downstream contracts consumer repair—with replan migrate, write the migrated v0.15 marker with planning authority and the exact upstream_version_blob_oid field plus immutable receipt, validate every gate, then use C-12 replan activate before implementation.

Run the first pristine migration against an isolated committed copy of all seven 2026-07-15-local-first-account-safety slices as one all-or-nothing public reachability proof, placing the exact corrected delta bytes on their canonical Planner ref and reproducing section 11's refreshed golden delta, Planner, receipt, transaction, protocol and activation blob/tree/commit OIDs without modifying the live downstream release.
out_of_scope: The engine does not decide requirements, decomposition, deferral rationale, Coach disposition, rollback need, replacement scope, or track grouping.
No started, implemented, verified, deferred, or shipped pre-v0.15 slice is reset or migrated in place.
The migration creates no synthetic report, invocation ID, fingerprint, implementation head, adjudication, PASS, or historical protocol evidence.

Applying the proven transformation to the live 2026-07-15-local-first-account-safety release occurs in a fresh Planner session only after this conformance release is integrated; it is tracked by sworn#121 and that release board.
Historical read-only protocol resolution belongs to S05-protocol-provenance-archive.

effort_complexity.rationale: This is a multi-ref, crash-recoverable planning transaction plus an all-or-nothing live migration whose core invariant is preserving opaque lifecycle authority.

risks.description: A re-plan seeded from release-wt overwrites newer owner-track reports or lifecycle transitions.
risks.mitigation: Pin the exact owner-track status blob, validate append-only C-05/C-06 history, and preserve the maintainability JSON subtree byte-for-byte except the one ratified rollback link.

risks.description: A partial migration leaves some local-first slices v0.15-active while sibling records still use the old shape.
risks.mitigation: Preflight all seven records and references before any commit, write one release-wt transaction and receipt, and expose no partial-selection path for the confirmed first migration.

risks.description: The CLI or MCP adapter smuggles unratified plan meaning into an apparently mechanical delta.
risks.mitigation: C-12 pins the human ratifier and exact before/after object identities; the engine validates declared mutations but refuses to derive or amend qualitative content.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN the built binary runs current-authority sworn replan apply --delta with operation replan or activation-policy sworn replan migrate --delta with operation protocol_migration, canonical committed delta bytes valid against plan-delta-v1, and every source/owner/target local-and-remote identity still matching THE SYSTEM SHALL seed from exact owner statuses, validate C-02/C-03/C-05/C-06/C-09 as applicable, apply only byte-exact declared mutations, create one deterministic release-wt transaction plus schema-valid receipt with derived planner identity/time, and reach fully_propagated topology only through declared refs; an exact C-08/S15 local invalidation SHALL be retained as target parent 1 while its unchanged remote advances from the declared parent, and all other target head pairs SHALL be equal (C-12, N-02, N-04, N-05, N-09, N-10, N-11).

WHILE a delta mutates a started slice, THE SYSTEM SHALL preserve its maintainability object byte-for-byte; ONLY WHEN C-12 explicitly ratifies a rollback_slice_id for a re_slice_required original MAY that one member differ, and the linked rollback SHALL be a new later slice that cannot be deferred (C-05, C-06, C-12, N-04, N-05).

WHEN a C-12 application or migrated activation restarts after its exact release-wt commit exists but before every declared ref contains the corresponding canonical propagation commit THE SYSTEM SHALL validate the immutable receipt and derived topology, replay zero planning decisions, create or push only missing deterministic transaction or activation commits from each pinned local/remote expected head, and preserve already-complete refs; the exact local-invalidation/remote-parent split is recoverable, but IF any source, local target, or remote target differs from its declared pre-state or canonical propagated state THE SYSTEM SHALL block without force (C-12, N-10).

WHEN an isolated SHA-1 repository exactly matches the source and every before blob in the complete schema-valid planning/local-first-migration-manifest.json delta whose migrated marker uses upstream_version_blob_oid THE SYSTEM SHALL use commit date bytes 1784124556 +1000 and exact unsigned subject-only commit construction to reproduce section 11's refreshed golden delta/Planner/receipt/transaction OIDs while installing all 17 after blobs, pass every listed gate, then reproduce the refreshed activated-protocol/tree/commit OIDs and reach fully_activated without synthetic execution evidence or any byte change to the live downstream release (C-02, C-03, C-12, N-02, N-11).

IF any selected migration record is started, non-planned, has actual files, reports, adjudication, rollback, non-pending verification, mismatched source blob, invalid schema, unresolved typed reference, trace failure, or ambiguity failure THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL block the entire migration, leave all seven records and refs unchanged, and create no receipt or synthetic v0.15 evidence (C-02, C-03, C-12, N-11).

WHEN MCP apply_plan_delta, migrate_plan_delta, or activate_migrated_plan receives the same respective C-12 bytes as replan apply, replan migrate, or replan activate THE SYSTEM SHALL call the corresponding same engine operation and produce identical transaction, receipt, propagation, activation, restart, and failure outcomes; each tool SHALL reject every other operation/edge class, while legacy MCP plan_release, create_slice, set_track, and update_intake SHALL be retired with Planner/delta guidance and zero authoritative release-record mutation (C-12, N-09).

IF ratified_at lacks a numeric RFC3339 offset, any mutation enters the canonical plan-deltas subtree, rollback_links repeat an original_slice_id or rollback_slice_id, or receipt_path equals any mutation path or canonical Planner delta path THEN every CLI and MCP C-12 adapter SHALL fail before creating an object, commit, receipt, or ref update and leave all source/target bytes unchanged (C-02, C-12, N-10).
